title: Recruitment Violation and Related Cases - Legal Assistance and Enforcement Measures
content:
This part defines illegal recruitment as acts of canvassing, enlisting, contracting, transporting, utilizing, hiring, or procuring workers for employment abroad by non-licensees or non-holders of authority, including offering or promising employment abroad for a fee to two or more persons. It enumerates specific acts constituting illegal recruitment, such as charging fees from applicant seafarers, publishing false information, misrepresentation to secure licenses, inducing seafarers to quit employment without valid cause, obstructing inspections, failure to submit required reports, altering employment contracts without approval, conflicts of interest involving travel agencies, withholding travel documents improperly, failure to deploy without valid reasons, and failure to reimburse expenses when deployment does not occur without fault of the seafarer.

The section outlines independent administrative actions separate from criminal proceedings, mandates the adoption of anti-illegal recruitment programs including victim assistance, surveillance, and education campaigns, and provides for free legal assistance to victims.

Procedures for receiving complaints, conducting surveillance, issuing and implementing closure orders against illegal recruitment establishments, and reporting on such actions are detailed. It also covers the institution of criminal actions, motions to lift closure orders, who may file such motions, grounds for lifting or reopening closed offices, appeals against denial of motions, and provisions for re-padlocking offices if illegal recruitment activities resume.

These measures are designed to enforce laws against illegal recruitment and protect seafarers and job seekers from exploitation.

metadata.source_file: 2003 POEA Rules on Overseas Employment of Seafarers.pdf
